frameworks.name: invariant linear probabilistic population code (ilPPC)
frameworks.one_line: Bayesian-optimal evidence accumulation via time-invariant linear sums over population activity.

frameworks.name: Bayesian optimal evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Extended to multisensory inputs with time-varying reliabilities.

core_question: How does the brain integrate multisensory inputs of time-varying reliability to make near-optimal decisions?
key_assumptions: Sensory inputs are encoded approximately as invariant linear PPCs
Optimality is defined in a Bayesian sense
Linear combinations across time and modalities can implement accumulation
manipulations: Vestibular vs visual modality of evidence
key_findings: LIP population activity is consistent with ilPPC-based integration
Accumulated evidence in LIP scales with vestibular acceleration and visual velocity
Sensory areas encode acceleration/velocity with close approximation to ilPPCs
interpretations: Simple time-invariant linear readouts of population activity suffice for near-optimal multisensory accumulation
ilPPC offers a biologically plausible solution to optimal multisensory decision making
